input.name: James
post: Shipped a since-last-post window and an in-app cohort feed.

Wins — James shipped two things on router-daybook today. The daily digest now pulls work since his last Router post instead of a fixed midnight-to-midnight day, anchored to the post's feed timestamp so it stays correct on any machine. He also added an in-app feed view that shows his posts and the cohort's in one list, newest first.

Struggles — The window math fought him. A missing checkpoint, a stale one, and a future timestamp from clock skew all had to clamp to a seven-day floor, and Codex stores sessions in per-day directories, so the collector had to walk each day in the range rather than do one bounds check. Getting the fallback label honest took a couple of passes.

Insight — Deriving the anchor from data that already exists — the Router's own record of your posts — beats writing a local checkpoint file that starts empty. The feed is the source of truth, so it just works on a fresh install.

Offering — @devon is "mixing up my own posts" with everyone else's in one timeline.¹ James just built that exact view and split the two with a single mine flag computed from the feed response — the server already tags each entry's handle, so it is a one-line filter, not a join. He can hand over the render function and the flag logic.

Asking — James wants the staging-timer design right before router-daybook posts go fully public. @mara has entries that "stay deletable for an hour"² before they publish; he needs to know how she set that hold duration and whether the timer lives server-side or client-side.

footnotes.excerpt: building an in-app timeline and I keep mixing up my own posts with everyone else's
entries sit in a hold state and stay deletable for an hour before they go public

feedEntries.content: Building an in-app timeline and I keep mixing up my own posts with everyone else's in the same list. Need a clean way to tell them apart.
Spent the day on staging-then-publish for our feed; entries sit in a hold state and stay deletable for an hour before they go public. Wiring the timer was fiddly.
Got an OAuth refresh loop working for our integrations.

input.workLog: router-daybook: changed collect to pull since last post via feed timestamp, clamped to 7-day floor, walked codex per-day dirs. Added in-app feed view with mine flag from cohortFeed. Files: transcripts.js, router.js, main.js, app.js, styles.css.
